name: Hawaii Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Hawaii - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: Hawaii Department of Commerce and Consumer Affairs (DCCA)

address.description:
Professional and Vocational Licensing Division
DCCA, PVL
P. O. Box 3469
Honolulu, HI 96801

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Hawaii SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost is approximately $175. Biennial renewal is $120.

mainEntity.name: Is Hawaii part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Hawaii has not enacted ASLP-IC legislation as of 2026.

mainEntity.name: Does Hawaii allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Hawaii permits telepractice but requires a valid Hawaii SLP license.

mainEntity.name: Does Hawaii have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in Hawaii. To work in Hawaii, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Hawaii?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 8 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Hawaii while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
